comment: Marketplace entries produced by Window E (RT-01..RT-05). The total integrator should merge these into .crabcode-plugin/marketplace.json. Each `source` points to ./plugins/<plugin-name> per migration rule §Marketplace Rules.

entries.name: hookify
entries.description: User-configurable CrabCode hooks driven by markdown rule files in .crabcode/hookify.*.local.md. Block or warn on Bash, Edit/Write/MultiEdit, Stop, and UserPromptSubmit events using regex or structured conditions.

entries.name: security-guidance
entries.description: Pre-write hook that warns about common security pitfalls when editing files: shell injection sinks, dynamic code evaluation, XSS sinks, unsafe deserialization, and risky GitHub Actions workflow inputs.

entries.name: explanatory-output-style
entries.description: SessionStart hook that injects an explanatory output style: the agent provides brief inline educational insights about implementation choices while it works.

entries.name: learning-output-style
entries.description: SessionStart hook that switches the agent into learning mode: surfaces meaningful 5-10 line code-write opportunities for the user and pairs them with inline educational insights.

entries.name: ralph-loop
entries.description: Iterative self-referential development loop. A Stop hook intercepts session exit and re-feeds the same prompt with iteration safety caps and an opt-in completion promise.

entries.name: discord
entries.description: Discord channel bridge for CrabCode. Self-contained MCP server with pairing, allowlist, and guild-channel access control. Managed by /discord:access skill.

entries.name: fakechat
entries.description: Localhost web UI for testing the CrabCode channel contract. Self-contained MCP server with stdio transport, attachment upload, and a minimal web client. No external service, no tokens, no access control.

entries.name: imessage
entries.description: iMessage channel bridge for CrabCode (macOS). Reads chat.db directly and sends via AppleScript; allowlist-based access control governs who can reach the agent.

entries.name: telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel bridge for CrabCode. Self-contained MCP server with pairing and allowlist access control. Managed by /telegram:access skill.
